data.content: Sometimes you don’t need a powerhouse laptop—you just need something that works when you need it. That’s where this refurbished Lenovo 300e Chromebook can be of use. It’s also currently on sale for just $79.99 (MSRP $284.99). This refurbished 2-in-1 Chromebook is a practical option as a backup computer. And it’s priced more like an accessory than a full laptop. It would work well as a low-risk, grab-and-go option. Whether you need something to check email on the couch, handle quick tasks while traveling, or keep as a backup when your primary laptop is out of commission, having a second device like this can make day-to-day tech a lot more flexible without adding much cost. Performance-wise, it’s really built for the basics. The Intel Celeron processor and 4GB of RAM handle everyday tasks like browsing, email, streaming, and working in web-based apps without issue. Chrome OS keeps things simple, with quick startup times and access to familiar tools like Google Docs, Sheets, and browser-based versions of Office apps. The Lenovo Chromebook 200e’s 11.6-inch display and lightweight design make it easy to carry around, while Wi-Fi and Bluetooth keep you connected wherever you are. It also doubles as a touchscreen device, which can be useful for quick navigation or casual gaming. With 32GB of storage, it leans on cloud-based workflows, which fits well with how most Chromebook users already operate. As a Grade B refurbished unit, it may show light cosmetic wear, but functionally, it’s built to handle the essentials. For a secondary device, that trade-off can make a lot of sense. Don’t miss getting a quality refurbished 2-in-1 Lenovo 300e Chromebook with touchscreen for just $79.99 (MSRP $284.99). Prices subject to change. data.content: Photographers looking to upgrade their editing workflow without paying a recurring subscription have a solid option right here. The Luminar Neo Lifetime Bundle is available for $79.99, down from its $682.00 suggested retail price, which is 88% off for a permanent license and a full suite of add-ons. Luminar Neo is an AI-powered photo editor built for both beginners and experienced photographers. The software handles common editing tasks faster than traditional tools, with features like sky replacement, portrait retouching, and background removal that run with minimal manual input. Unlike many editing platforms that lock key features behind subscription tiers, this deal covers lifetime desktop access in a single purchase. What’s included This Luminar Neo Lifetime Bundle goes beyond just the core app. Alongside the Luminar Neo lifetime license, buyers get a video course covering creative photo editing techniques specific to the software. There are also six add-on packs included: Light Reflections Overlay, Color Harmony LUTs, Wintertime Overlay, Frosty Winter LUTs, Tranquil Dawn Skies, and Tender Blushing Skies. For photographers who do seasonal or atmospheric work, having those assets baked into one purchase adds real practical value. The LUT and overlay packs alone carry a combined listed value of $454.00. Paired with the $199.00 software license and the $59.00 video course, the math works out to $682.00 in total value across all eight items. Verified buyers on the product page rated it 4.7 out of 5 stars, with reviewers citing the AI features, sky replacement tools, and overall image quality as highlights. One buyer noted the desktop version improved noticeably on the mobile app they had already been using. For photographers tired of paying month after month for editing software, the Luminar Neo Lifetime Bundle at $79.99 (reg. $682) is worth a look. Prices subject to change. data.content: Online ads have gotten aggressive. Pop-ups, autoplay videos, trackers following you across the internet—it adds up and overtakes pretty fast, especially if you’ve got multiple devices in the house. This lifetime AdGuard Family Plan promotion offers a one-time purchase of $15.97 that covers up to 9 devices, making it less of a personal tool and more of a full household solution. Instead of relying on browser extensions alone, AdGuard works at a deeper level to filter out ads, block trackers, and reduce exposure to malicious sites. That means fewer interruptions, faster page loads, and a cleaner overall browsing experience. Another point worth noting is how AdGuard is designed to work across different platforms without locking you into one device type. Whether you’re using Android or iOS, the experience stays consistent, so you’re not managing separate tools for each system. That flexibility makes it easier to roll out across a household. AdGuard’s Family Plan also includes built-in parental controls, which can help manage what younger users can access online. You can block specific categories of content without needing extra apps or complicated setups. One important note: this isn’t a VPN. It won’t encrypt your traffic or change your location, but it does focus heavily on privacy by limiting tracking and preventing unwanted scripts from loading in the first place. The main appeal here is simplicity. You set it up once, apply it across devices, and it just works in the background. For a one-time $15.97 through May 3 (MSRP $169.99), the AdGuard Family Plan is a straightforward way to clean up your entire household’s internet experience without adding another subscription. data.content: Anyone who has tried learning a language with AI tools in those AI apps has probably noticed the gap: they can generate responses, but they don’t always teach you how people actually speak. Babbel Language Learning takes a different, more human approach. And you can currently get lifetime access to all languages for a one-time $159 (MSRP $646.20) with the StackSocial code LEARN. Babbel’s courses were created by more than 150 linguists and language educators, not generated on the fly. The focus is on real-world conversations—ordering food, asking for directions, or navigating everyday situations—so you’re learning language the way it’s actually used. That includes cultural context, tone, and phrasing that automated tools often miss. The structure is also designed for consistency. Babble’s lessons are short (10 to 15 minutes), practical, and organized by skill level, so you can build confidence over time without feeling overwhelmed. Speech recognition helps with pronunciation, while review sessions reinforce what you’ve learned. Another factor to consider is consistency. Many AI tools are designed for quick answers, which can make learning feel scattered over time. The Babbel platform takes a more structured approach, building lessons that connect and progress logically so you’re not just picking up random phrases but actually developing a usable skill set. That kind of continuity can make a noticeable difference when you’re trying to retain what you learn and apply it in real situations. This lifetime subscription covers 14 languages and more than 10,000 hours of content, accessible across mobile and desktop with offline support. It’s built for long-term use, whether you’re learning for travel, work, or personal growth. For anyone looking for a more human, conversation-first way to learn a language, it’s a straightforward alternative to AI-only tools. You can currently get lifetime access to Babbel Language Learning for a one-time $159 (MSRP $646.20) using code LEARN via StackSocial. Prices subject to change. data.content: Cybersecurity isn’t just an IT niche anymore. It shows up in everyday life, from protecting personal data to understanding how businesses handle risk. That’s part of what makes platforms like InfoSec4TC relevant to more than just tech professionals. This InfoSec4TC Platinum Membership offers lifetime access to more than 90 self-paced courses covering areas such as ethical hacking, governance and compliance (GRC), and certification prep for exams like the CISSP. The structure is flexible, so you can move through the material at your own pace without needing to commit to a fixed schedule. What stands out is how the content connects to real-world use. Whether you’re looking to strengthen your resume, pivot into a new field, or just better understand how security impacts your daily work, these courses are designed to be practical rather than purely theoretical. This InfoSec4TC Platinum Membership also includes supporting resources like updated exam questions, a student portal, and access to private groups for discussion and learning. A one-time career consulting session is included as well, which can help map out next steps if you’re considering a shift into cybersecurity. With this membership, you get lifetime access to a full library of cybersecurity courses, along with future updates as new content is added. It also includes real-world project training. Since access is lifetime, it’s something you can return to over time—whether you’re building foundational knowledge or keeping up with changes in the field. data.content: If you’ve been thinking about joining Costco, this is your sign. Right now, you can get a 1-Year Gold Star Membership for $65 and receive a $20 Digital Costco Shop Card* when you sign up online. That means your first trip may already have a little head start. When most people think of Costco, they think of value, convenience, and quality—whether that’s stocking up on essentials, filling up at the Costco Gas Station, or scheduling an appointment at the Costco Tire Center while you peruse the aisles. The Gold Star Membership includes a complimentary Household Card, which allows an individual in your home (18 years and older) to enjoy the same benefits. It’s valid at every Costco location (over 500 nationwide) and online at Costco.com. What really stands out about this 1-year Costco Gold Star Membership promotion is just how many ways it can support your daily needs. In one visit, you might grab groceries, find a new pair of shoes, grab those office essentials you constantly need, schedule a tire rotation, and fill your tank—all while knowing you’re getting excellent quality and service. It’s a fantastic time to join. You’ll be able to enjoy summer finds to support all of your upcoming adventures. And when the summer winds down and the fall and winter approach, you’ll be able to see all the seasonal products and goodies available to make the most of the season. Once you complete your online redemption, Costco will email your $20 Digital Costco Shop Card* within two weeks. Just be sure to redeem online before heading to a warehouse location to finalize your membership. Don’t miss getting a 1-Year Gold Star Membership for $65 + $20 Digital Costco Shop Card* while you can. Prices subject to change. *To receive a Digital Costco Shop Card, you must provide a valid email address and set up auto-renewal of your Costco membership on a Visa® card at the time of sign-up. If you elect not to enroll in auto-renewal at the time of sign-up, incentives will not be emailed. Valid only for new members and those whose memberships (Primary and Household) have expired at least 18 months or more. Valid only for nonmembers for their first year of membership. Not valid for renewal or upgrade of an existing membership. Promotion may not be combined with any other promotion. Digital Costco Shop Card will be emailed to the email address provided by the Primary Member at time of sign-up within 2 weeks to qualifying members. Digital Costco Shop Card is not redeemable for cash, except as required by law. Costco is not liable for incentives not received due to entry of an invalid address during sign-up. Digital Costco Shop Cards are not accepted at U.S. or Canada Food Court. Neither Costco Wholesale Corporation nor its affiliates are responsible for use of the card without your permission.Use the provided single-use promo code when entering your payment information. A Costco Gold Star Membership is $65 a year. An Executive Membership is an additional $65 upgrade fee a year. Each membership includes one free Household Card. May be subject to sales tax. Costco accepts all Visa cards, as well as cash, checks, debit/ATM cards, EBT and Costco Shop Cards. Departments and product selection may vary. (Note: You will see a $0.01 deduction on your membership cost after the promo code is entered. This indicates that your promo code has been successfully applied).
